name: code_section
description: Read a named code section (function, method, struct, interface, enum, trait, impl block, var/const, class) from a source file. Automatically finds the section by name and determines its boundaries by brace matching (for Go, Rust, TypeScript, C/C++) or indentation analysis (for Python). Shows line numbers. Supports Rust (fn, struct, enum, trait, impl, type, mod, const, static), Go (func, type, var, const), Python (def, class - with proper indentation-based block detection), TypeScript (function, class, interface, type, enum), C/C++ (struct, class, union, functions), and other brace-delimited languages.
when_to_use: Use when you need to inspect a specific function, struct, enum, trait, class, or other named code section but don't know its exact line range. Replaces 2-3 separate grep/read steps (first grep for definition, then nl+sed to read line range). Particularly useful when working with large files where manually finding line numbers is tedious. Supports Rust-specific patterns like 'pub(crate) fn', 'async fn', 'pub struct', 'impl Trait for Type'. For Python files, uses indentation-based block detection to find the end of classes and functions (instead of brace matching which doesn't work for Python).

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Path to the source file (e.g., evaluator/functions.go)

parameters.name: section_name
parameters.description: Name of the function, struct, enum, trait, impl, interface, var, const, or class to find (e.g., 'run', 'Environment', 'User', 'handle_forward')

examples.expected: Lines of the requireFn function definition and body, with line numbers.
Lines of the BeginEval function with line numbers.
Lines of the Environment struct definition with line numbers.
Lines of the run() function definition from the Rust VM module, with line numbers.
Lines of the Vm struct definition with line numbers.
Lines of the Context struct definition from a Rust source file.
Lines of the User class definition from a Python SQLAlchemy models file, with proper indentation-based block detection showing the entire class body.
Lines of the handle_forward function definition from a Python email handler file, showing the complete function body.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-3 separate steps (grep to find definition line, nl+sed to read specific range, possibly multiple attempts if range is wrong) with a single script call. The brace-matching logic eliminates trial-and-error when determining where a function/block ends. For Python, the indentation-based block detection properly captures the full class or function body without needing to estimate line ranges. Broad language support means fewer round trips trying different grep patterns across languages.
